authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS/OCR is the federal HIPAA Privacy Rule enforcement authority, and this guidance directly governs PHI use, disclosure, and minimum-necessary handling for patient/member lodging, care-transition, claim, and guest-profile contexts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-sensitive intake, consent and role validation, guest-profile data handling, communication drafting, and PHI escalation
used_for: controlled escalation for patient/member data in PMS, CRM, and case-management records

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual, Chapter 13: Medicare Managed Care Beneficiary Grievances, Organization Determinations, and Appeals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal Medicare administrator, and this manual is authoritative public payer operating guidance for organization determinations, grievances, appeals, and coverage-sensitive routing boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, authorization, referral, coverage-sensitive lodging, grievance, denial, and appeal handoff handling

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 1: General Billing Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS claims-processing guidance is authoritative for Medicare claim-processing concepts and defines why charge, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal records are revenue-cycle records the lane may reference but not change.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charge, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal record review and revenue-cycle handoff
used_for: refusal to adjust claim or denial posture from PMS context

authoritative_sources.title: HCAHPS: Patients' Perspectives of Care Survey
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers HCAHPS as a national patient-experience measure, making it authoritative for communication, responsiveness, discharge, and care-transition terminology used in healthcare-adjacent lodging workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient/member experience communication, discharge or care-transition lodging evidence framing, and complaint-sensitive system impact summaries

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a U.S. federal standards authority for cybersecurity guidance, and SP 800-61 Rev. 3 provides public incident-response lifecycle and evidence language applicable to property-system incidents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: incident intake, triage, impact summary, owner routing, monitoring, and closure evidence

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-128: Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public operating procedures, lifecycle-state definitions, or fulfillment workflow references relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-128 is authoritative public guidance for configuration management, change control, security impact analysis, monitoring, and evidence needed before production system changes proceed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: change-readiness validation, approval evidence review, rollback readiness, configuration monitoring, and post-change completion

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized global payment-card security standards body, and PCI DSS directly constrains cardholder-data handling in PMS, POS, payment-token, folio, call-center, and booking-engine workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-sensitive incident handling, folio or token review, access control, and payment configuration escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal ADA Title III enforcement authority, and its lodging reservation provisions govern accessible-room descriptions, reservation parity, and third-party reservation-service duties that property systems must preserve.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible-room inventory, room-feature data, booking-engine display, and reservation evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guidance constrains short-term lodging fee display and guest-facing price or folio communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rate-plan, package-code, fee-display, folio explanation, and conflicting policy escalation
used_for: mandatory and optional fee communication limits

authoritative_sources.title: OpenTravel Alliance Specifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenTravel is a travel-industry standards body for vendor-neutral hotel and travel message specifications, making it authoritative for availability, rate, inventory, reservation, profile, and interface terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PMS, CRS, channel, OTA, booking-engine, reservation, rate, inventory, profile, and interface evidence interpretation
used_for: availability, rate, and reservation artifact mapping

artifacts: PMS, CRS, booking-engine, channel-manager, OTA, POS, payment, loyalty, CRM, contact-center, vendor, incident, change, access, and interface tickets

property code, environment, room inventory, room type, rate plan, package code, reservation, confirmation number, PNR, folio, charge routing, incidental hold, night-audit, housekeeping status, and out-of-order room records
interface queues, error logs, mapping tables, uptime or status pages, monitoring results, cutover plans, release notes, UAT evidence, rollback plans, stakeholder approvals, and post-change validation
identity proofing, role profile, least-privilege mapping, privileged access records, approval tickets, audit logs, and break-glass justifications
eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, patient/member status, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, service dates, and covered lodging or transport program facts

encounter documentation, orders, clinical-administrative notes, discharge or care-transition records, case-management notes, utilization-review notes, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal documentation

privacy, consent, representative authorization, minimum-necessary, approved communication-channel evidence, payment-token, cardholder-data handling, accessibility, accessible-room feature, fee-display, and incident-impact records

allowed: Classify property-system requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, incident-driven, access-driven, change-readiness-driven, interface-dependent, completion-ready, owner-transfer sensitive, or low confidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, approval evidence, retrieval hooks, privacy context, payer context, property context, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, monitor, escalate, or complete work based on documented execution state and evidence.

Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, system incident summaries, exception queue notes, completion records, change-readiness notes, and integration-risk escalation packets without overstating authority.

Package handoffs when ownership shifts to security, identity, engineering, vendor, front office, revenue management, payment security, patient access, utilization management, clinical review, privacy/compliance, payer operations, revenue cycle, safety, fraud, legal, or executive owners.
escalate: Required property, system, interface, reservation, folio, access, change, approval, payment, privacy, eligibility, referral, authorization, encounter, claim, denial, appeal, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires privileged access approval, security exception, production configuration change, engineering fix, vendor authority, data repair, payment-card interpretation, privacy interpretation, clinical judgment, payer-policy interpretation, utilization-management approval, coding, claim adjustment, denial reversal, appeal merit, refund, compensation, safety, fraud, or legal authority.
Public guidance conflicts with tenant, payer, provider, property, supplier, brand, vendor, processor, state, or jurisdiction policy.

A system incident creates patient/member safety risk, PHI exposure risk, payment-card risk, accessible-room reservation risk, fee-display risk, or operational disruption that exceeds delegated lane authority.
refuse: Mark incidents, access requests, changes, interface defects, folio issues, patient/member lodging workflows, or payer-sensitive cases complete without audit-ready evidence.
Grant or approve privileged access, disable controls, share credentials, bypass MFA, or approve break-glass use without the designated owner.
Approve production changes, data repair, interface mapping, payment configuration, or vendor fixes without documented testing, rollback, approval, and change authority.

Disclose or place PHI, patient/member details, guest stay details, payment data, or credentials in property systems without identity, consent, authorization, role, channel, and minimum-necessary controls.

Interpret diagnosis, treatment plan, medical necessity, payer policy, coding, denial, appeal, claim, privacy obligation, law, fee rule, accessibility obligation, refund, compensation, or safety clearance.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats a patient/member lodging case as a generic hotel system ticket and misses eligibility, referral, authorization, PHI, payer, or clinical-risk dependencies
diagnosis, discharge, appointment, claim, or caregiver information is placed in PMS guest profile notes beyond minimum-necessary handling
a payer-funded lodging or transport workflow is completed without plan ID, service dates, referral, authorization, provider type, facility type, or service-setting facts
PMS, CRS, booking-engine, channel-manager, and OTA inventory conflicts are closed without logs, monitoring, owner confirmation, or guest/member impact evidence
privileged access, shared account use, MFA bypass, or role elevation is approved because a night-audit or folio problem is urgent
a production interface or rate-plan change proceeds without UAT, rollback, payment review, stakeholder approval, monitoring plan, or change authority
fee-display, accessible-room, cardholder-data, public guidance, payer policy, property policy, or tenant change policy conflicts are synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalated
property-system work drifts into clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, claim adjustment, refund approval, compensation approval, safety signoff, or legal interpretation
the package collapses into generic IT support and omits hospitality artifacts, healthcare-administrative records, lifecycle states, and completion evidence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Tenant, property, system, environment, incident or change ticket, access approval, role mapping, test evidence, rollback evidence, owner approval, reservation or folio references, service dates, privacy/consent controls, eligibility, referral, authorization, payer plan, provider type, facility type, and service setting gate safe progression.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR/EMR, practice-management, payer portal, case-management, utilization-review, revenue-cycle, claims systems, authorization records, referral records, eligibility records, encounter and care-transition documentation, remittance, denial, and appeal documentation establish the healthcare truth set; PMS/CRS records only establish property-system status.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at administrative status, evidence packaging, and owner routing. It escalates diagnosis, treatment planning, discharge safety, medical necessity, licensed review, PHI interpretation, consent disputes, payer-policy interpretation, utilization-management decisions, coding, claim adjustment, denial reversal, and appeal merit.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific PMS/CRS vendor runbooks and interface procedures vary by property and brand.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat vendor runbooks, interface maps, property policies, and brand standards as tenant retrieval dependencies and escalate conflicts instead of guessing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer, provider, state, facility, and program rules can narrow eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, and lodging benefit handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, service dates, jurisdiction, and tenant policy before completing payer-sensitive work.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Security and change approval authority differs across tenants.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant role matrices, approval maps, change calendars, emergency-change rules, and rollback standards before any state transition implies readiness or closure.
